ogTitle: Potts Hill vs Potts Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Potts Hill and Potts Point.
heading: Potts Hill vs Potts Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Potts Hill, New South Wales and Potts Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Potts Point has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to Potts Hill (51/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Potts Hill or Potts Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Potts Hill has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Potts Point scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Potts Hill scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Potts Point scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Potts Hill or Potts Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Potts Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 3.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
